LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NAOMI CAROLINO-AU'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW A REASONABLE PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS OF HER CONTRACT CLAIM FOR BREACH OF COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE. AMBIGUITY WHETHER COVENANT ENCOMPASSES TWO GEOGRAPHIC LOCATIONS OR JUST THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED AS TO ADDRESS OF SAN BRUNO OFFICE AND DENIED AS TO GOOGLE MAP AND NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION MEMO. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PHUONG NGUYEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED IN PART, AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION. NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT DECLINES TO INTERPRET THE NON-COMPETE CLAUSE AT THIS EARLY STAGE TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FAILS. (COUNSEL TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Off Calendar

Defendant Phuong Nguyen'S Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Phuong Nguyen's Motion to Strike portions of the unverified Complaint. Appearances: Jennifer M. Ellis, Esq. of Goldman Law Firm (415) 435-5500, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Michael M. Ching, Esq. of Cannata Ching & O'Toole LLP (415) 409-8900, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike is granted in part, as to paragraph 6 with ten days leave to amend. Motion is denied as to remaining issues. (Counsel to prepare Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Plaintiff Naomi Carolino-Au'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Naomi Carolino-Au's Motion for Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Jennifer M. Ellis, Esq. of Goldman Law Firm (415) 435-5500, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Michael M. Ching, Esq. of Cannata Ching & O'Toole LLP (415) 409-8900, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff fails to show a reasonable probability of success on the merits of her contract claim for Breach of Covenant not to compete. Ambiguity whether covenant encompasses two geographic locations or just the City and County of San Francisco. Judicial notice is granted as to address of San Bruno office and denied as to Google map and National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration memo. (302/CWW)

Defendant Phuong Nguyen'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Phuong Nguyen's Demurrer to Complaint. Appearances: Jennifer M. Ellis, Esq. of Goldman Law Firm (415) 435-5500, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Michael M. Ching, Esq. of Cannata Ching & O'Toole LLP (415) 409-8900, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained in part, and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to 9th cause of action. No reasonable possibility that plaintiff can state a cause of action. Demurrer is overruled as to the 8th cause of action. The Court declines to interpret the non-compete clause at this early stage to determine whether plaintiff's claim fails. (Counsel to prepare Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
